response.justification: While Szabo demonstrates solid backend systems engineering and current engagement with agentic-AI infrastructure at re:cinq, critical gaps prevent a CTO recommendation. The role requires production multi-agent AI systems at scale (resume mentions MCP tooling and 'agent frameworks' but provides no details on autonomous decision-making, multi-agent coordination, feedback loops, or production failure modes), fintech-infrastructure expertise (last relevant work was pricing optimization 15+ years ago; no credit systems, payment flows, regulatory compliance, or embedded finance mentioned), and demonstrated large-scale org leadership (15-year tenure at re:cinq as Staff Software Engineer suggests individual contributor track, with no evidence of scaling teams from 10 to 30+). Strong engineering fundamentals exist, but executive-level fintech domain depth and proven agentic-AI production operations are missing.
strengths: 30+ years of pragmatic systems engineering across web, mobile, and backend; Dunnhumby experience with microservices handling millions of RPS demonstrates distributed-systems fluency
Current agentic-AI work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agent frameworks) shows engagement with cutting-edge domain; multilingual and low-latency systems expertise (async I/O, real-time metadata)
Strong educational output (workshops, open-source frameworks like Fluent-Asserts and Trial, talks) suggests ability to develop technical leaders and communicate complex concepts
concerns: Agentic-AI production credentials lack specificity: mentions 'frameworks supporting agentic workflows' with no detail on multi-agent coordination, shared memory, stateful agent reasoning, evaluation pipelines, or cost modeling

Fintech infrastructure expertise largely absent: most recent fintech work was Optilyz (2007–2011) pricing optimization; no credit systems, payment flows, regulatory compliance, or high-stakes AI operations mentioned
No demonstrated large-scale team leadership: 15-year tenure as Staff Software Engineer at re:cinq without evidence of building org from 10 to 30+ people, hiring/mentoring at scale, or org governance

key_factors.factor: Lack of large-scale org leadership narrative
